metadata.name: Saddle — Quilled Charger saddled by a 2/2 creature, designation cleared at cleanup
metadata.description: P1 controls Quilled Charger (4/3 Porcupine Mount, Saddle 2) and Solemn Simulacrum (2/2). During P1's precombat main phase with an empty stack, P1 activates Saddle 2 by tapping Solemn Simulacrum (power 2 >= saddle threshold 2). The saddle ability goes on the stack (CR 702.171a: Saddle is an activated ability). After both players pass priority the ability resolves: Quilled Charger becomes saddled until end of turn (CR 702.171b). P1 then passes priority through all remaining steps to advance to the cleanup step of the same turn, where the saddled designation is cleared (CR 702.171b). A dispute notes that the assertion DSL does not expose the is_saddled flag, so the script verifies the interaction through observable side-effects: Solemn Simulacrum is tapped after activation, the saddle ability appears on the stack (count 1), and the stack empties after resolution.
metadata.generation_notes: Quilled Charger oracle text (card definition verified at crates/engine/src/cards/defs/quilled_charger.rs): 4/3 Porcupine Mount, Saddle 2 keyword (AbilityDefinition::Keyword(KeywordAbility::Saddle(2))). Card is in the Available Cards list as it exists in the engine. Solemn Simulacrum is used as the saddling creature (power 2 >= saddle threshold 2). Both are placed on the battlefield in initial_state at turn_number 4 so neither has summoning sickness (ruling: summoning sickness does not prevent saddling, same as Crew; but turn 4 avoids any ambiguity). The saddle_mount action in the replay harness uses the 'convoke' field for the list of saddling creature names (reuses the Crew pattern per replay_harness.rs:936-944). The 'card' field names the mount. After SaddleMount command is processed: (1) Solemn Simulacrum is tapped immediately as a cost (CR 602.2: costs paid at activation time); (2) the saddle ability is placed on the stack (CR 602.2a: activated abilities use the stack); (3) is_saddled remains false until the ability resolves (verified in saddle.rs unit test). After both players pass priority the ability resolves: Quilled Charger becomes saddled (is_saddled = true, CR 702.171a). The saddled designation lasts until end of turn (CR 702.171b). To advance to cleanup, the script passes priority through EndOfCombat (no combat in this scenario), PostcombatMain, and Ending phases using priority_rounds. At cleanup, the engine clears is_saddled per CR 702.171b. ASSERTION LIMITATION: The check_assertions harness (tests/script_replay.rs) only exposes 'permanent.<card>.tapped' and 'permanent.<card>.counters.<type>' for individual permanent state. The is_saddled boolean flag on GameObject is not accessible via the assertion DSL. A dispute records this gap. The script asserts the observable side-effects: Solemn Simulacrum tapped after activation, stack count = 1 after activation, stack is_empty after resolution. To confirm is_saddled behavior, see crates/engine/tests/saddle.rs test_saddle_basic_mount_becomes_saddled and test_saddle_cleared_at_cleanup. CR 702.171a: 'Saddle N means: Tap any number of other untapped creatures you control with total power N or greater: This permanent becomes saddled until end of turn. Activate only as a sorcery.' CR 702.171b: 'The saddled designation is cleared at the beginning of the cleanup step.'

disputes.description: The script assertion DSL (check_assertions in tests/script_replay.rs) does not support 'permanent.<card>.is_saddled' or any path that reads the is_saddled boolean on GameObject. Only 'permanent.<card>.tapped' and 'permanent.<card>.counters.<type>' are supported for individual permanents. As a result, the key assertions 'Quilled Charger is_saddled = true after saddle resolves' and 'Quilled Charger is_saddled = false after cleanup' cannot be expressed in this script. The script instead verifies observable side-effects: saddling creature tapped, ability on stack, stack empties after resolution. The full is_saddled behavior is covered by unit tests in crates/engine/tests/saddle.rs (test_saddle_basic_mount_becomes_saddled, test_saddle_cleared_at_cleanup). To resolve: extend check_assertions to handle 'permanent.<card>.is_saddled' as a boolean path, then add the two missing assertions.

script.step_note: P1 activates Saddle 2 on Quilled Charger by tapping Solemn Simulacrum (power 2 >= 2). Saddle ability goes on the stack. Both players pass. Ability resolves: Quilled Charger becomes saddled until end of turn.

actions.description: Initial: Quilled Charger and Solemn Simulacrum untapped on p1 battlefield. Stack is empty. Life totals 40/40.
actions.note: Both permanents were placed in initial_state at turn 4 (builder sets has_summoning_sickness: false for initial-state permanents). Quilled Charger is a 4/3 Mount with Saddle 2. Solemn Simulacrum is a 2/2 artifact creature — its power 2 exactly meets the Saddle 2 threshold. Stack is empty; P1 holds priority in precombat_main (sorcery speed required for Saddle per CR 702.171a).

CR 702.171a: P1 activates Saddle 2 ability of Quilled Charger. Taps Solemn Simulacrum (power 2 >= saddle threshold 2) as the saddling cost. CR 602.2: Costs are paid at activation time — Solemn Simulacrum is tapped immediately. The saddle ability is placed onto the stack (CR 602.2a: activated abilities use the stack). P1 holds priority after activation (CR 116.3). The harness uses the 'convoke' field for saddling creature names per replay_harness.rs translate_player_action 'saddle_mount' branch.

actions.description: After saddle activation: saddle ability on stack (count 1), Solemn Simulacrum is tapped (cost paid at activation), Quilled Charger still untapped.
actions.note: CR 602.2: Tapping Solemn Simulacrum is a cost paid at activation time, before the ability is placed on the stack. The saddle ability now sits on the stack (count = 1). Quilled Charger itself is not tapped — the saddle ability's effect (becoming saddled) has not resolved yet. Per unit test test_saddle_basic_mount_becomes_saddled: is_saddled remains false until the ability resolves.
CR 116.3: P1 holds priority after activation; p1 passes, then p2 passes. Stack resolves top object (saddle ability). CR 405.5: LIFO — saddle ability is the only stack object.

resolution.note: CR 702.171a: The saddle ability resolves — Quilled Charger becomes saddled until end of turn. The saddled designation is a boolean flag on the GameObject (is_saddled = true), not a card type change. CR 702.171b: The designation is cleared at the beginning of the cleanup step.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Activated ability resolves. Quilled Charger gains the saddled designation (is_saddled = true) until end of turn. No ETB trigger fires (Quilled Charger was already on the battlefield). NOTE: is_saddled cannot be asserted via the script DSL — see disputes entry. The saddled condition enables Quilled Charger's attack trigger ('gets +1/+2 and gains menace until end of turn') if it attacks this turn, though that trigger is currently deferred from the card definition per TODO in quilled_charger.rs.

actions.description: After saddle ability resolves: stack is empty, Solemn Simulacrum remains tapped, Quilled Charger remains untapped on battlefield. Quilled Charger is now saddled (is_saddled = true — not assertable via DSL, see dispute).
actions.note: CR 702.171a: Saddle ability resolved — Quilled Charger is saddled. Solemn Simulacrum remains tapped (cost paid at activation; untapping happens at p1's next untap step). Stack is empty. is_saddled flag is set to true on the engine's GameObject but cannot be asserted via the current assertion DSL.

CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority with empty stack in PreCombatMain → advance through BeginningOfCombat, DeclareAttackers, DeclareBlockers, CombatDamage, EndOfCombat, PostcombatMain, and into the Ending phase (since P1 does not declare any attackers). Priority rounds continue advancing the turn.

script.step_note: P1's turn advances to the Ending phase (End step then Cleanup). At the beginning of cleanup, the engine clears the saddled designation on Quilled Charger per CR 702.171b. Solemn Simulacrum's tapped state persists until P1's next untap step.

actions.note: CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority in the End step with an empty stack → advance to the Cleanup step. CR 702.171b: At the beginning of the Cleanup step, the saddled designation on Quilled Charger is cleared (is_saddled set to false). No triggered abilities from cleanup (no damage to clear, no hand size reduction needed here).

actions.description: Final state after cleanup: Quilled Charger is no longer saddled (is_saddled = false — not assertable via DSL), Solemn Simulacrum remains tapped (untaps at P1's next untap step). Both creatures remain on battlefield. Life totals unchanged at 40/40.
actions.note: CR 702.171b: The saddled designation is cleared at the beginning of the cleanup step. Quilled Charger's is_saddled flag is now false (not assertable via current DSL — see dispute). Solemn Simulacrum remains tapped; it will untap at the beginning of P1's next turn (CR 502.1). No combat occurred, so no damage was dealt and life totals remain 40/40. This verifies the full saddle lifecycle: activation → stack → resolution → saddled-until-cleanup → cleared.
